narrative: In a small community, residents contribute to a shared well that supplies water to everyone, including people they do not know personally. A resident must decide how much of their own water allocation to put into the common fund versus keeping it for their immediate household and close neighbors.
high_response: I will contribute a fair share to the common well. Even people I do not know personally rely on it, and building trust that the resource will be available for everyone strengthens the whole community over time.
low_response: I will keep most of my water for my own household and the people I know well. I can trust them to look after me in return, and I understand their needs better than those of strangers.
